title: wangenheimia disticha
intro: Wangenheimia disticha, also known as 'two-ranked wood-burning bush', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Wangenheimia disticha include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and burns. Herbal preparations of Wangenheimia disticha include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
